title: hypericum australe
intro: Hypericum australe, also known as the southern St. John's Wort, is a perennial plant native to Australia and New Zealand, belonging to the St. John's Wort family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of hypericin and hyperforin. The health benefits of Hypericum australe include its potential to treat anxiety, depression, and skin conditions, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling. Hypericum australe can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
